ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic lobster food product class; no specific slipper lobster grilled preparation exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to slipper lobster (Thenus orientalis) grilled preparation. Crustaceans parent group used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for slipper lobster grilled food product. SNOMED CT does not cover this specific prepared seafood item.

serving.description: 1 whole slipper lobster (approximately 300 g edible portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of grilled spicy slipper lobster consists of one whole slipper lobster weighing approximately 300 g of edible flesh, served with a spicy marinade and often accompanied by steamed rice.

nutrient_source.title: Lobster, cooked, moist heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15147); adapted with spice marinade adjustment using IMR Malaysia MyFCD grilled crustacean reference
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / IMR Malaysia
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base macros derived from USDA SR Legacy cooked lobster (NDB 15147 / FDC ID 175180). Sodium adjusted upward to reflect spicy marinade (soy sauce, oyster sauce) typical of Malaysian Zi Char preparation, consistent with IMR Malaysia MyFCD grilled seafood with sauce references. Fat adjusted slightly upward for oil used in grilling. Values represent a reasonable estimate for the composite grilled spicy preparation per 100 g edible portion.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; shellfish and crustacean analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grilled spicy slipper lobster. GI estimated at 5 based on analogous crustacean / shellfish data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Shellfish and crustaceans are very low-carbohydrate foods with negligible glycemic impact; GI values for similar seafood preparations are consistently in the 0–10 range. Glycemic load per 300 g serving calculated as (5 × 1.5 × 300/100) / 100 = 0.23, rounded to 0.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium from soy sauce and oyster sauce marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size or request reduced-sauce preparation, as the spicy marinade contributes approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g, which can represent a significant fraction of the 1500–2000 mg daily sodium target recommended for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and negligible glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled spicy slipper lobster is a high-protein, very low-carbohydrate food with a glycemic index of approximately 5 and a glycemic load of less than 0.5 per serving, making it a suitable protein source for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided the accompanying sauces and rice portions are monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate dietary cholesterol and elevated sodium from marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While slipper lobster provides lean protein and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids, the cholesterol content of approximately 145 mg per 100 g and the high sodium from the spicy marinade warrant moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease, particularly those managing LDL cholesterol or blood pressure.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Spicy Slipper Lobster is a popular Malaysian and Singaporean Zi Char seafood dish featuring the slipper lobster (Thenus orientalis), a flat-bodied crustacean distinct from clawed lobsters, which is marinated in a bold spicy sauce typically comprising chilli, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, soy sauce, and oyster sauce, then grilled over high heat until the shell is charred and the flesh is tender and juicy. The dish is a staple of Malaysian seafood restaurants and Singaporean Zi Char stalls, prized for its sweet, firm, and slightly smoky flesh that absorbs the aromatic spice marinade. Nutritionally, slipper lobster is a lean, high-protein seafood with approximately 20.5 g protein and only 2.8 g fat per 100 g, making it a nutrient-dense choice. The primary clinical consideration is the elevated sodium content from the marinade (approximately 480 mg per 100 g), which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic impact is negligible due to the very low carbohydrate content, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is typically served as a shared main course in a seafood restaurant setting, accompanied by steamed white rice and stir-fried vegetables.
common_user_queries: Is slipper lobster good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for grilled spicy slipper lobster exists in any approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked lobster data with marinade-based sodium and fat adjustments consistent with Malaysian Zi Char preparation conventions. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base crustacean data is from a high-quality approved source and the adaptations are methodologically defensible.
notes: 1. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy lobster entry and is not reliably estimated for this preparation. 2. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for cooked lobster and cannot be reliably estimated for the composite dish. 3. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero): lobster and the marinade ingredients do not contain industrially produced trans fats; this is naturally zero for this food. 4. Sodium value of 480 mg per 100 g is an upward adaptation from the plain cooked lobster baseline (~380 mg) to account for soy sauce and oyster sauce marinade typical of Malaysian Zi Char grilled seafood preparations. 5. GI of 5 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database shellfish/crustacean data; no direct GI measurement for slipper lobster exists in the literature. 6. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes use closest parent matches as no specific slipper lobster grilled preparation codes exist in either ontology. 7. The dish is served at Marina Seafood House - Boat Quay (field survey data source) at SGD 42.00, consistent with premium whole-lobster restaurant pricing in Singapore.
